quotes.text: I'll be honest with you — I had a staffer explain to me what a 'pivot table' was three separate times, and each time I walked away feeling worse about myself than the time before.
quotes.speaker: Joe Biden
quotes.context: Remarks at a Democratic Governors Association technology briefing, Washington D.C., March 2019
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Biden's self-deprecating humor about being behind the technological curve is well-documented, and this fits his pattern of candid, slightly rambling admissions about feeling outpaced. The subtle giveaway is that 'pivot table' is just specific enough to feel planted.

quotes.text: I tuned into Joe Rogan yesterday and heard Bernie Sanders' economics, Barack Obama's foreign policy, and Ro Khanna's conspiracy theories. And then I realized JD Vance was talking.

quotes.text: Freedom is the right to be wrong, not the right to do wrong.

quotes.text: Someone on my team put together a forty-page briefing document on proposed changes to agricultural subsidy classifications. I read every page. I'm not sure that's normal. My wife is certain it isn't.
quotes.speaker: Mike Pence
quotes.context: Remarks at the Indiana State Fair Agriculture Roundtable, Indianapolis, Indiana, August 2015
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Pence cultivated a reputation as an earnest, detail-oriented Midwesterner, and a self-aware joke about his own diligence reads true to that persona. The slight tell is the wife punchline, which wraps it a shade too neatly.

quotes.text: The thing about a filibuster that they don't put in the textbooks is what it smells like in there after hour nine. That's the real test of conviction.

quotes.context: Interview on The Hugh Hewitt Show, nationally syndicated radio, March 2015
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Paul has spoken publicly about his 2013 filibuster with a mix of pride and dark humor, and a sensory, unglamorous detail like this matches his libertarian-maverick tendency to puncture institutional solemnity. The giveaway is 'hour nine,' which is specific in a way that invites fact-checking.
